paper.title: Scaling Behavior of Discrete Diffusion Language Models

checks.question: Is the codebase structured so CPU-only or single-GPU inference is feasible, or is it fundamentally tied to TPU / JAX distributed training? Cite the dependency declarations or hardware-specific code paths.
checks.notes: Training is fundamentally JAX/EasyDeL with multi-device sharding (FSDP+TP, multihost sync), but a standalone PyTorch HuggingFace model (modeling_gidd_hf.py) and a pytorch-inference notebook exist, so CPU-only / single-GPU inference is feasible via the torch path even though training is not.

checks.question: Does the repo provide hyperparameter sweeps or scaling recipes for each model size (25M / 85M / 170M / 302M / 567M / 3B / 10B), or must the user hand-tune for each scale?

evidence.snippet: parser.add_argument(\"--num_layers\", type=int, default=8, help=\"Number of layers in the model.\")
parser.add_argument(\"--hidden_size\", type=int, default=512, help=\"Hidden size of the model.\")
parser.add_argument(\"--lr\", type=float, default=0.5, help=\"Learning rate for the optimizer.\")
parser.add_argument(\"--max_training_steps\", type=int, default=100_000, help=\"Maximum number of training steps.\")

checks.notes: The repo exposes a single argparse CLI with one default set of hyperparameters and has no configs/ or scripts/ directory providing per-size (25M/85M/.../10B) sweeps or scaling recipes — users must pass CLI flags to scale each run.

checks.question: Are pretrained model checkpoints published externally (HuggingFace or similar) so inference-only evaluation is feasible without a full training run? Cite the reference if any.

evidence.snippet: The 3B and 10B models are available as converted PyTorch models on HuggingFace and can be used as follows:

checks.notes: Pretrained 3B/10B GIDD checkpoints are published as PyTorch models on HuggingFace under `dvruette/gidd-*` and loadable via `AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained`, enabling inference-only evaluation.

checks.question: Is the training data (Nemotron-CC) pinned to a specific version / preprocessing recipe in the repo, or does the code assume the user reproduces the data pipeline independently?

evidence.snippet: The exact data split that was used for training, already pre-tokenized and pre-shuffled, is available here: https://huggingface.co/datasets/dvruette/gidd-nemotron-cc-pretok
Make sure to download this data to your local storage/your GCS bucket and pass the path containing the dataset via the `DATA_FILES` environment variable.

checks.notes: The repo pins the training data to a specific pre-tokenized, pre-shuffled Nemotron-CC split hosted on HuggingFace (dvruette/gidd-nemotron-cc-pretok), consumed via the DATA_FILES env var — users download the pinned artifact rather than reproducing the pipeline.

checks.question: Does the repo expose the interpolation parameter between masked and uniform diffusion (the paper's main methodological novelty) as a first-class configurable in the training config?

evidence.snippet: hybrid_mixing_scale: float = field(default=1.0, metadata={\"help\": \"Sigmoid scale for the hybrid mixing schedule.\"})
hybrid_mixing_shift: float = field(default=0.0, metadata={\"help\": \"Sigmoid shift for the hybrid mixing schedule.\"})

checks.notes: The `DiffusionConfig` TrainingArguments expose `hybrid_mixing_scale`/`hybrid_mixing_shift` which parameterize the sigmoid α interpolating between uniform and masked priors in `HybridMixingDistribution.pi_lambda`, making the masked/uniform interpolation a first-class training config field.
